back_translation: The LORD said to Noah, "Enter the ark, you and all your household, because I have seen that you are righteous before Me in this generation.
Take with you seven pairs of every kind of clean animal, male and female, and one pair of every kind of unclean animal, male and female,
and seven pairs of every kind of bird of the air, male and female, to preserve their offspring on all the earth.
For seven days from now I will send rain on the earth for forty days and forty nights, and I will sweep away every living thing I have made from the face of the ground."
Noah did everything that the LORD had commanded him.
At that time Noah was 600 years old, when the floodwaters came upon the earth.
Noah, his sons, his wife, and his sons' wives entered the ark to escape the floodwaters.
Both clean and unclean animals, birds, and every kind of crawling creature on the ground
came to Noah in the ark, two by two, male and female, as God had commanded Noah.
After seven days the floodwaters came upon the earth.
In the 600th year of Noah's life, on the 17th day of the second month, on that very day all the fountains of the great deep burst forth, and the gates of the heavens opened.
Rain fell upon the earth for forty days and forty nights.
On that very day Noah, his three sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, Noah's wife, and the three wives of his sons entered the ark.
They and every kind of wild animal, every kind of livestock, every kind of crawling creature on the ground, every kind of bird, every winged creature.
They came to Noah in the ark in pairs, from every kind of creature with the breath of life.
Those that came—male and female of every kind of creature—entered as God had commanded him. Then the LORD shut the door for him.
The Flood was upon the earth for forty days, and the waters rose and lifted the ark up from the earth.
The waters increased greatly and surged on the earth, and the ark floated on the surface of the waters.
Finally, the floodwaters rose exceedingly upon the earth, until all the high mountains under the heavens were covered by water.
The waters rose to a height of about 15 cubits above the mountaintops, and all the mountains were submerged.
Every living creature that moved on the earth perished—birds, livestock, wild animals, every kind of swarming creature on the earth, and all mankind.
Every life that had breath in its nostrils and was on dry land perished completely.

Every living thing on the face of the ground was swept away—mankind, livestock, crawling creatures, and birds of the sky—they were blotted out from the earth, and only Noah and those with him in the ark remained.
The waters covered the earth for 150 days.
